track_description: Modest exogenous improvement in residual kiln and grinding intensity on non-CCS cement methods, separated from clinker-factor, fuel-switch, and CCS pathway changes.
evidence_summary: Australian cement evidence supports only a modest autonomous efficiency drift on residual kiln and grinding energy demand once the same drift is made explicit instead of left embedded in pathway-method rows.
derivation_method: Rebaseline the conventional and low-clinker cement rows on the affected thermal and electrical inputs, then apply a common autonomous track so explicit efficiency attribution preserves the current modeled pathway.
double_counting_guardrail: Applies only to the conventional and low-clinker methods. CCS remains excluded because its interactions are route-specific, and the base rows are rebaselined so the explicit track does not double count.
review_notes: Kept modest so the autonomous drift complements rather than recreates clinker-factor alternative-fuel or CCS pathway changes.
